fixes_applied_this_iteration: Floor colors: dramatically different per room type - IT dark blue-gray, break warm wood, ER clinical white, lab green-tint, lobby marble, records olive carpet
Floor patterns: boosted opacity 3-5x - IT raised panels with vent holes, break parquet wood grain, ER non-slip diamonds, lab grid dots + drain lines, records carpet weave, lobby marble veining
Room tint overlays: boosted from 0.04 to 0.05-0.08 with per-room color tuning

Wall decorations: added to ALL rooms - ER red cross + emergency stripe, lab biohazard placard + safety sign, IT status lights + RESTRICTED label, break menu board, records section labels, lobby hospital H logo, reception clock
Multi-tile furniture: now tiles sprites across full obstacle area instead of single centered sprite on brown rectangle
NPC labels: added dark background + increased stroke thickness for readability on any floor color
Furniture shadows: boosted from 0.10 to 0.15 opacity for better grounding

hospital_entrance.notes: Marble floor + H logo give grand lobby feel. Simple room with 1 NPC keeps it straightforward. Satisfaction slightly lower - only one NPC interaction, limited gameplay arc.

reception.notes: Warm beige floor, welcome sign, clock. 3 NPCs with distinct roles. Sign-in sheet zone is clever. Privacy screens create visual zones. Good first real gameplay room.

break_room.notes: Warmest room - wood parquet floor, amber tint, menu board. Vending machines, couches, coffee station create cozy vibe. Social gate is unique mechanic. 5 distinct NPCs. Emotional tone hits 9 - genuinely feels like a break room.

lab.notes: Green-tinted floor with drain lines gives clean room feel. Lab bench spans center properly. Biohazard placard and safety sign on walls. Observation gate on results_printout teaches investigate-first. Distinct from all other rooms.

records_room.notes: Olive carpet floor, section labels on wall. Dense filing cabinets create aisles. Choice gate is strongest gameplay moment. Readability slightly lower because screenshot shows choice overlay blocking room view.

it_office.notes: Dark raised-floor panels with vent holes - most distinct floor. Server racks tiled across full walls. Status lights and RESTRICTED label. Cable channels on floor. Emotional tone hits 9 - unmistakably a server room.

er.notes: Clinical white-blue floor with non-slip diamonds. Red cross and emergency stripe on walls. Curtain partitions, crash cart, IV stands tiled properly. Red urgent tint. Emotional tone hits 9 - feels like a real ER.

remaining_to_reach_9: Lens 3: records_room readability - choice overlay blocks room view in screenshots, less of a gameplay issue
Lens 7: hospital_entrance gameplay satisfaction - only 1 NPC, limited gameplay arc
Lens 7: per-room 'moment' — each room needs one standout memorable interaction beyond just talking
Per-room polish: more ambient detail props that tell micro-stories without gameplay function
Audio: room-specific ambient sounds and interaction audio feedback still missing
assessment: Major jump from 7.5 to 8.0. Every room is now visually distinct - you could identify each room from the floor alone. IT office and ER floors are dramatically different from reception and break room. Multi-tile furniture fills space properly. NPC labels are readable on any background. Wall decorations give each room identity. Three rooms hit 9 on emotional tone (break room, IT office, ER). Reaching 9 across all lenses would require content/gameplay additions beyond visual improvements.
